title: Using the AI feature to create a summary of individual item updates
excerpt: Is it possible for the AI feature to create summaries of work/effort/etc. from the item updates in a board? I provide a weekly recap of what I’ve worked on, am currently working on, and plan to work on to my director. Cu…

posts.author: JIvan (Jennifer Ivan)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible for the AI feature to create summaries of work/effort/etc. from the item updates in a board? I provide a weekly recap of what I’ve worked on, am currently working on, and plan to work on to my director. Currently I’m using a board to manage all the tasks, and each item is updated as it’s “touched”. Then, I basically duplicate that information into a Monday doc, export it to a PDF file and email it to him. He is a board owner but does not actually use Monday much at all and prefers to receive the information in this format. It would be extremely helpful if the weekly recap could be automatically generated using AI.

posts.author: rochelleg (Rochelle Gebelius)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey! I totally get where you’re coming from. That was the exact workflow that inspired me to build RecapIQ.

It connects to your boards and automatically pulls updates, status changes, and progress into a clean weekly recap. Each Recap has natural-toned AI summary, overdue and stalled items, and what’s been completed, and it emails everything directly to whoever needs it. No manual doc or PDF required.

It’s live on the marketplace now if you want to take a look. I built it specifically for teams like yours where leadership prefers a simple weekly digest over logging into Monday.

11920×960 135 KB
